title: Phenomenon 38
wikitext:
{{VoidedTheme}}{{Blurbackground|file=Sudden Unrealism.webp}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Phenomenon 38: Sudden Unrealism}} {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Rubik:ital,wght@0,300..900;1,300..900&display=swap');}}{{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Rubik+Broken+Fax&family=Rubik+Dirt&family=Rubik+Maze&family=Rubik+Maze&display=swap');}} {{Phenomenon Class|danger=2|effectiveness=5|frequency=3}} ==
Description
== {{Blurimage|file=385.png|caption=One of the most popular photographs of Sudden Unrealism, taken in [[Level 385]].}} '''Phenomenon 38''', also known as '''Sudden Unrealism''', is a common phenomenon that occurs all across the Backrooms, known for making objects or locations unrealistic. At times, '''Sudden Unrealism''' can come off as abstract, uncanny, or surrealistic. Those who experience '''Sudden Unrealism''' often describe their experiences as disorienting and fascinating. Manifestations of '''Sudden Unrealism''' are best described as unbelievable. But also oddly real, as in being immersive and belonging in the environment, unlike common hallucinations. Objects shift into something entirely different while everything around them remains unharmed. One room could be normal, and the other could have fractions of '''Sudden Unrealism'''. Some manifestations can be nightmarish or kaleidoscopic, as everything around one transforms into a mere husk of what it was. Affected objects may even become unrecognizable at times. Despite this, manifestations of '''Sudden Unrealism''' largely differ from person to person and level to level. {{Blurimage|file=Sudden Unrealistic Level 0.webp|caption=A rare image of Level 0 undergoing [[Phenomenon 38|Sudden Unrealism]].}} Some levels are dominated by '''Sudden Unrealism''', with all or most of their expanse undergoing the phenomenon. Examples include [[Level 385]], [[Level 99]], [[Level 381]], [[Level 984]], and [[Level 115]]. It is thought that some triggers, like moments of sadness or fear, chemical reactions, or the sheer disbelief of being in the Backrooms, cause the brain to receive a stimuli influx, causing overactivity in sensory regions. These stimuli are interpreted as originating externally due to abnormal connectivity or functionality of the feedforward network. This especially happens to people who have recently entered the Backrooms. Their lack of memories of the Backrooms causes the brain to create even more absurd imagery. {{Blurimage|file=Sudden Unrealism.webp|caption=Sudden Unrealism in [[Level 4]].}} '''Sudden Unrealism''' also extends to technology, mainly video devices like cell phones and cameras. These most commonly include pictures being displayed incorrectly or in low quality and glitched sounds. In more extreme cases, rather than just glitched sounds, the device may produce new, abstract sounds or even stop loading audio and/or imagery entirely. Unfortunately, it is unclear why this occurs. Although it is known that imagery glitches are caused by hardware issues and, most commonly, overheating. Manifestations of '''Sudden Unrealism''' can cause confusion and some extreme behaviors. The sheer sight of this phenomenon can cause the weakest of individuals to behave irrationally or be driven to heavy disorientation, nausea, or [[Phenomenon 33|Unnatural Recklessness]], usually due to sheer disbelief. Most commonly, since it defies their beliefs and knowledge. Typically, the effects of '''Sudden Unrealism''' wear off about 10-20 minutes after manifesting. Normally, it disappears suddenly, most commonly after blinking or leaving a room.  '''Sudden Unrealism''' may also disappear if any triggers wear out.{{Blurimage|file=Sudden Unrealism 2.png|caption=A [[Object 17|Lubik’s Cube]] suffering heavy Sudden Unrealism.}} '''Sudden Unrealism''' affects single objects more drastically than entire locations. This is because the phenomenon is focused only on that object; consequently, unrealism is more centered, but when manifested in multiple objects, it would be more spread around. ==
Discovery
== '''Sudden Unrealism''' was first discovered in [[Level 99]], initially thought to be an exclusive property of that level. However, users around [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]] would begin posting and discussing their experiences with this phenomenon outside of [[Level 99]]. The phenomenon itself helped these posts circulate forums through display glitches. It would also circulate through the many curious photographs of '''Sudden Unrealism'''. It would not take long until entire teams were being formed to research this phenomenon. Eventually, discoveries were made as [[M.E.G.|larger]] [[Backroom Colonists|groups]] stepped into the situation. ==
Addenda
==
---- August 27th Interviewee reports that, before the phenomenon, there were no notable indications of its occurring. It was a normal day; the interviewee was exploring [[Level 4]]. They say that, after opening a door, they were greeted by a CGI-esque room. The interviewee expressed their confusion at that moment. At first, they thought it was some sort of hallucination. But after pulling out a camera to record it, they noticed that the camera displayed exactly what the interviewee was seeing. The interviewee initially shrugged and assumed it was a visual anomaly. They report that this phenomenon wouldn't wear off until they left that region entirely. October 2nd Interviewee #2 reports an interaction of '''Sudden Unrealism''' with a crystal of [[Object 15|Firesalt]] in [[Level 117]]. According to the interviewee, they were exploring [[Level 117]] to find [[Level 117#Luvidium Crystals|Luvidium Crystals]] and other minerals to sell to [[M.E.G.|certain groups]]. They say that they found a crystal of [[Object 15|Firesalt]] lying in a corner. As the interviewee carefully grabbed it, they blinked. Once their eyes opened, the [[Object 15|Firesalt]] in their hands became a simple, orange polygonal shape, unrecognizable to anyone. The interviewee admits that they dropped the [[Object 15|Firesalt]] due to the shock. They confess that they had never seen '''Sudden Unrealism''' in person. The [[Object 15|Firesalt]] shattered and exploded in the ground, and pieces of it flew to the interviewee's legs. After the event, they had second-degree burns for some months and weren't able to explore for a long while. Because of this, the interviewee has been trying to remain more calm in such situations to avoid injuries like such.{{Blurimage|file=Sudden Unrealism Firesalt 2.png|caption=A rough representation of how the [[Object 15|Firesalt]] might have looked like.|align=center}}
